window_summary: This window tracks the recent nushell/nushell release stream from version 0.94.0 through 0.113.1, including major feature releases and patch releases with bug fixes and contributor acknowledgments.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: nushell/nushell
Release: 0.94.0
Tag: 0.94.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-05-28T19:41:32Z; created_at: 2024-05-28T19:04:09Z
Release notes:
This is the 0.94.0 release of Nushell. You can learn more about this release here: http://www.nushell.sh/blog/2024-05-28-nushell_0_94_0.html
For convenience, we are providing full builds for Windows, Linux, and macOS. Be sure you have the requirements to enable all capabilities: https://www.nushell.sh/book/installation.html#dependencies
This release was made possible by PR contributions from @amtoine, @app/dependabot, @ayax79, @cablehead, @cptpiepmatz, @devyn, @ExaltedBagel, @fdncred, @FilipAndersson245, @francesco-gaglione, @hustcer, @IanManske, @kubouch, @lavafroth, @maxim-uvarov, @NotTheDr01ds, @rgwood, @rtpg, @sholderbach, @Skyppex, @szepeviktor, @WindSoilder, @YizhePKU
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the nushell/nushell release 0.94.0, the project announces the 0.94.0 release of Nushell, providing full builds for Windows, Linux, and macOS. The release was made possible by PR contributions from multiple contributors.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: nushell/nushell
Release: 0.94.2
Tag: 0.94.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-06-03T19:55:23Z; created_at: 2024-06-03T07:38:55Z
Release notes:
This is the 0.94.2 release of Nushell. You can learn more about this release here: https://www.nushell.sh/blog/2024-06-03-nushell_0_94_2.html
For convenience, we are providing full builds for Windows, Linux, and macOS. Be sure you have the requirements to enable all capabilities: https://www.nushell.sh/book/installation.html#dependencies
In this release, we're releasing two bugfixes:
* Fix external command name parsing with backslashes, and add tests by @devyn in https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/13027
* Fix `run_external::expand_glob()` to return paths that are PWD-relative but reflect the original intent by @devyn in https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/13028
This release was made possible by PR contributions from @devyn.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the nushell/nushell release 0.94.2, the project announces the 0.94.2 release of Nushell, providing full builds for Windows, Linux, and macOS. This release includes two bugfixes: fixing external command name parsing with backslashes and adding tests, and fixing run_external::expand_glob() to return paths that are PWD-relative but reflect the original intent. The release was made possible by PR contributions from @devyn.

questions.query_text: In the nushell/nushell releases, which release includes a note that version 0.97.0 was never released and that 0.97.1 is the next major version after 0.96.1?

answer_support.answer_text: Note that 0.97.0 was never released; this is the next major version after 0.96.1.

questions.query_text: Across the nushell/nushell releases, how does the contributor list in release 0.94.2 differ from the contributor list in release 0.94.1?

options.text: Both releases credit the same set of contributors.

answer_support.answer_text: The release was made possible by PR contributions from @devyn.
The release was made possible by PR contributions from @devyn, @fdncred, @IanManske, and @WindSoilder.

questions.query_text: In the nushell/nushell release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that includes two bugfixes for external command name parsing and glob expansion, (2) a release that notes that the previous minor version was never released, (3) a release that is a patch release crediting only @IanManske.

answer_support.answer_text: This release includes two bugfixes: fixing external command name parsing with backslashes and adding tests, and fixing run_external::expand_glob() to return paths that are PWD-relative but reflect the original intent.
Note that 0.97.0 was never released; this is the next major version after 0.96.1.
The release was made possible by PR contributions from @IanManske.
